organizational_affiliations.label: College of Natural and Behavioral Sciences

biography: Chris is a graduate of the Christopher Newport Information Science program. After spending several years continuing his graduate education while also moving around as a Military spouse, Chris has returned to teach at CNU.
education_entries: Ph D in Information Systems/IS Cyber Security, Dakota State University
MS in Computer Science, Georgia Institute of Technology
MIT in Information Technology, Virginia Polytechnic Institute and State University
research_interests: Information Security (Passwords) Online Communities (Wikipedia) Healthcare and Information Systems
teaching_interests: Information Systems/Information Science related classes such as systems analysis and design, database design.
publications: Conference Proceeding (2025). Enabling Novel Authentication Interfaces in AR/VR While Providing Legacy Support . AMCIS. Issue, 2025.
Conference Proceeding (2025). An Evaluation of Cybersecurity Education Trends in Public State Universities . AMCIS. Issue, 2025.
Conference Proceeding (2025). ASSESSING THE MATURITY OF CYBERSECURITY EDUCATION IN VIRGINIA AND IMPACT OF STATE LEVEL INVESTMENT . Southern Association for Information Systems (SAIS).

Conference Proceeding (2025). EXPERIMENTAL EVALUATION OF PERCEIVED MEMORABILITY IN KNOWLEDGE BASED AUTHENTICATION FOR VIRTUAL AND AUGMENTED REALITY . Southern Association for Information Systems (SAIS).
Conference Proceeding (2023). Formative Validity of A Novel Authentication Artifact for Augmented and Virtual Realities . Americas Conference on Information Systems.

Conference Proceeding (2022). A Decade in Review: Aligning Information Systems Security (ISS) with the NICE framework . Americas Conference on Information Systems (AMCIS). Volume, 28th Annual Meeting.

Conference Proceeding (2022). Reconceptualizing Knowledge Based Authentication for Augmented and Virtual Reality Contexts . Americas Conference on Information Systems (AMCIS). Volume, 28th Annual Meeting.
Journal Article, Academic Journal (2022). AN EXPLORATION OF WIKIPEDIA CONTRIBUTIONS IN THE ERA OF COVID-19 . Journal of the Southern Association for Information Systems.
Conference Proceeding (2022). ACCOUNTS THAT NEVER EXPIRE: AN EXPLORATION INTO PRIVILEGED ACCOUNTS ON WIKIPEDIA . Southern Association for Information Systems. Issue, 24th Annual Meeting.

Conference Proceeding Personalized Route Navigation System: Utilizing Available Static and Live Data for Preference-Based Recommendation . 2021 IEEE Global Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Internet of Things (G. Pages, 6.
Conference Proceeding (2021). Reducing Risk of Password Reuse through Random Character Requirements and Image Prompting . Midwest Association for Information Systems (MWAIS).

Journal Article, Academic Journal (2021). A Decade in Review: A Meta-Analysis of the level of analysis and the subjects for Information Systems (IS) Cybersecurity Research . Journal of the Southern Association for Information Systems.
Journal Article, Academic Journal Reducing Risk of Password Reuse through Random Character Requirements and Image Prompting . Journal of the Southern Association for Information Systems.
Conference Proceeding (2020). QUANTIFYING PROGRAM OFFERINGS WITH A CYBERSECURITY EDUCATION MATURITY MODEL . Southern Association for Information Systems. Issue, 23rd.
Conference Proceeding (2020). A FRAMEWORK FOR DESCRIBING ALTERNATIVE KEYBOARD STRUCTURES IN AUGMENTED REALITY . Southern Association for Information Systems. Volume, 23rd.
Conference Proceeding (2019). A Framework for Cybersecurity Gap Analysis in Higher Education . Southern Association for Information Systems. Issue, 22.
Conference Proceeding An Exploration of Countermeasures for Augmented Reality Shoulder Surfing Attacks . Southern Association for Information Systems. Volume, 22.

Conference Proceeding (2017). THE DISCOVERABILITY OF PASSWORD ENTRY USING VIRTUAL KEYBOARDS IN AN AUGMENTED REALITY WEARABLE: AN INITIAL PROOF OF CONCEPT . Southern Association for Information Systems. Issue, 22.

Presentations: Christopher Kreider, Lonnie Cheney, Patrick Mero, 31st Americas Conference on Information Systems (AMCIS), "An Evaluation of Cybersecurity Education Trends in Public State Universities," Montreal, CA. (2025)
